text: Rachel nan bui ke, “Uwumbɔr le toor maabɔr tii mi. Uma le ŋun maameel le ki tii mi ubijabo.” Nima pu na, u nan yin u ke Dann.
translated: Rachel gave birth and said, "God has judged my case. He has also heard my voice and given me a son." Therefore they called him Dan.

ref: Finally, God remembered Rachel—he answered her prayer by giving her a son. “God has taken away my disgrace,” she said.
text: U nan dek lipuul le ki ma ubijabo. Le u bui ke, “Uwumbɔr chuu nyan maanimɔɔn le ki tii mi ubijabo.
translated: You should go to the forest and bring me a monkey. And you should say, "God has given me wisdom and made me a monkey.

ref: Then Laban made Jacob keep the rest of the sheep and goats at a distance of three days' journey.
text: ki cha bi chuun iwiin itaa ki buen timoor ni, nin chee daa na. Le Jakob nan gaa Labann aapiih i gur na ki joo i.
translated: They were still talking with them when Rachel arrived with her father's sheep, for she was a shepherdess. When Jacob saw Rachel, Laban's daughter, he went and rolled the stone away.

ref: Jacob heard that Laban's sons were complaining, “Jacob is now a rich man, and he got everything he owns from our father.”
text: Le Jakob ŋun ke Labann aajapɔtiib bui ke, “Jakob gaa tite aawan mɔmɔk a. Waah kpa tiwan nimɔk na nyan ni tite yaan ponn ni la.”
translated: When Jacob heard Laban's sons' words saying, "Jacob has taken all our father's wealth. And he has acquired all this wealth from what belonged to our father."

ref: and he told them: Your father isn't as friendly with me as he used to be, but the God my ancestors worshiped has been on my side.
text: Baah fuu ni na le u bui bi ke, “M bee ke nite aa ki gee maabɔr ke waah nan gee mi njan pu na; tɔ, nte Aawumbɔr bi m chee.
translated: If anyone asks you why you are doing this, tell them, "The Lord needs it and will return it soon"; or, "The Lord has sent me."

ref: long years to earn your two daughters and another
text: Jakob nan ŋmann Labann, le kaa cha u nyi ke u ga nya udo.
translated: Jacob stayed with Laban, and they agreed that he would work for him for seven years.

ref: If you find that any one of us has taken your idols, I'll have that person killed. Let your relatives be witnesses. Show me what belongs to you, and you can take it back.” Jacob did not realize that Rachel had stolen the household idols.
text: Aa mu yaa kan unii u joo saawaa na kan, ku udaan ngbaan. Ti mɔmɔk sɔ do, timi aanib bee le ga li ye biseeraadam, lik m ni maanib aawan mɔmɔk ponn ni, ki nyan saawan aah ye ni na mɔmɔk.” Jakob aa nan nyi ke upuu Rachel le sun ute Labann aawaa.
translated: If you will go with me to my father's house, I will give you a great reward. We will agree together, you and I, that you will serve me for seven years, and I will give you my younger daughter to marry, the one you love." Jacob agreed to this with Laban's daughter Rachel whom he loved.

ref: Laban said to Jacob, “Leah and Rachel are my daughters, and their children belong to me. All these sheep you are taking are really mine too. In fact, everything you have belongs to me. But there is nothing I can do to keep my daughters and their children.
text: Labann nan bui Jakob ke, “Mpiibim bimina ye mbisatiib la, le min le yeh baabim ni tiwakor timina mɔmɔk. Mbamɔn, tiwan nimɔk bi do na ye maawan la. Le maah kaan ŋmaa gaa mbisatiib ni baabim na kan,
translated: Laban said to Jacob, "Because you are my relative, should you work for me for nothing? Tell me what your wages should be. Now, I have two daughters. The older one is called Leah and the younger one is Rachel.

ref: Then Laban said: If you mistreat my daughters or marry other women, I may not know about it, but remember, God is watching us!
text: Labann nan ki bui ke, “Aa yaa ŋa mbisatiib bee bakaa, bee aa yaa ki yoor bipiib kpee bi pu kan, hali m yaa kaa nyi kan, teer ke Uwumbɔr waa si.
translated: "The people said to him, "If you are the Christ or the king, come down from the cross and save yourself, and if you do that, then we will believe that God sent you."

ref: Jacob got up in the middle of the night and took his wives, his eleven children, and everything he owned across to the other side of the Jabbok River for safety.
text: Waah nan di bi puur doo na, le u ki di waawangol mɔmɔk mu di puur,
translated: When they are about to harvest, they don't allow weeds to grow in the farm,

ref: Then the man asked, “What is your name?” “Jacob,” he answered.
text: Ntuun ngbaan nan baa u ke, “Bi yin si ke ba?” Le u bui ke, “Jakob.”
translated: The man asked him, "What is your name?" And he answered, "Jacob."

ref: The sun was coming up as Jacob was leaving Peniel. He was limping because he had been struck on the hip,
text: Le nwiin aah puu buyoonn na Jakob nyan Peniel ki cha, le ki nan deeni ukpiik pu.
translated: As the sun rose upon him as Jacob passed Peniel, he was limping on his thigh.

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: See ni mɔmɔk bui u ke, ‘Een, saanaagbiija Jakob le bi kɔnkɔni do ki choo na.’” Jakob nan dakl ke, “Nibaakan Esɔ yaa kan ipiin ngbaan kan, u ga li gee mi, aan ti yaa kan tɔb kan, u ga di cha pinn mi.”
translated: "If you ask him, he will say, 'Yes, the flocks belong to your servant Jacob, and he is sending them as a gift to you.'" Jacob then said, "I will appease Esau with the gift that goes before me, and afterward when I see his face, perhaps he will accept me."

ref: The sun was coming up as Jacob was leaving Peniel. He was limping because he had been struck on the hip,
text: Le nwiin aah puu buyoonn na Jakob nyan Peniel ki cha, le ki nan deeni ukpiik pu.
translated: As the sun rose upon him when Jacob passed Peniel, he was limping on his thigh.

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: Buyoonn bitutum ngbaan gir ni Jakob chee na, bi nan bui u ke, “Ti buen aakpel Esɔ do, le u gor ki choo ke ni nan tooh tɔb nsan ni. U choo ki joo ni bijab ikui inaa la.”
translated: When the young men arrived where Jacob was, they told him, "We have found Esau coming, and he is coming with four hundred men. He is coming with his warriors who are with him."

ref: But you have promised that I would be a success and that someday it will be as hard to count my descendants as it is to count the grains of sand along the seashore.
text: Teer ke aa nan puu tipuur tii mi ke aa ga li kii ki ter mi tiwan mɔmɔk ni, ki ga cha m maa pɔr, le n‑yaabitiib ga li wiir ke litambol li dɔ limɔɔl aagbaan na, le ubaa aan ŋmaa kahn bi.”
translated: The land that you will give to the people so that you may be able to establish my name there, they will build me a house, and the priests will be there as witnesses that the altar has been placed there, and no one will be able to destroy it.

ref: And Jacob told them to be sure to say that he was right behind them. Jacob hoped the gifts would make Esau friendly, so Esau would be glad to see him when they met.
text: See ni mɔmɔk bui u ke, ‘Een, saanaagbiija Jakob le bi kɔnkɔni do ki choo na.’” Jakob nan dakl ke, “Nibaakan Esɔ yaa kan ipiin ngbaan kan, u ga li gee mi, aan ti yaa kan tɔb kan, u ga di cha pinn mi.”
translated: He told his father, "Father, the firstborn of Jacob is the one who is supposed to eat it." Jacob then answered, "My brother Esau is a hairy man, but I am a smooth man."

ref: But Esau ran toward Jacob and hugged and kissed him. Then the two brothers started crying.
text: Esɔ ma nan san ni, le ki nan bub chuu u, le ki moor utakpiln, le bimɔk bilee nan wii tɔb pu.
translated: If they do not agree, they will fight each other, they will kill people, and many things will be destroyed.

ref: Esau asked Jacob, “Why did you send those herds I met along the road?” “Master,” Jacob answered, “I sent them so you would be friendly to me.”
text: Le Esɔ ki baa ke, “Tiwakor ti m ba puen ton ti na nyan ni la?” Le Jakob bui ke, “Ipiin i m tii si na le bi na.”
translated: And Esau said, "What is the meaning of all this company that I met?" And Jacob said, "To find favor in the sight of my lord."

ref: The land where he camped was owned by the descendants of Hamor, the father of Shechem. So Jacob paid them
text: le ki di tikunabr aamombil nkub daa nkpaakon ngbaan, Hamor u nan ye Sekem aate na, aayaabitiib chee.
translated: When the young men of the city, Hamor's sons and Shechem's brothers, heard this, they were very angry.

ref: While Jacob and his family were traveling through Canaan, God terrified the people in the towns so much that no one dared bother them.
text: Nee aapuwɔb le Jakob ni udo yaab nan piin ki nyan cha, le ijawaan sakpiin chuu binib bi aatim nan bi gbɔk nima chee na, le baa kaa bi paan bi pu.
translated: And the sons of Jacob came from the field when they heard it, and the men were grieved and very angry because he had done a disgraceful thing in Israel by lying with Jacob's daughter, for such a thing ought not to be done.

ref: Jacob built an altar there and called it “God of Bethel,” because that was the place where God had appeared to him when he was running from Esau.
text: Le u maa kitork aabimbiln nima chee, le ki yin kitiŋ ngbaan ke Betel Aawumbɔr; ba pu? Uwumbɔr nan di ubaa mɔk Jakob nima chee, n‑yoonn mu u nan san ukpel chee na.
translated: When they left Bethel, while they were still some distance from Ephrath, Rachel began to give birth, and she had great difficulty in childbirth.

ref: I will give you the land that I promised Abraham and Isaac, and it will belong to your family forever.
text: M ga di kitiŋ ki m nan di tii aayaaja Abraham ni aate Aisak na di tii si, ni aayaabitiib bi ga paan ni aapuwɔb na.”
translated: I will give the land that I promised to give to your ancestor Abraham and to his son Isaac, and your descendants will possess it with the foreigners."

ref: The clans that descended from Esau and Oholibamah the daughter of Anah were Jeush, Jalam, and Korah.
text: Le ŋinibol ŋi mu aayimbil paa na ye Esɔ aapuu Oholibama u nan ye Ana aabisal na aayaabitiib: Jeus, ni Jalam, ni Kora.
translated: And these are the sons of Esau's wife Oholibamah, daughter of Anah, granddaughter of Zibeon: Jeush, and Jalam, and Korah.

ref: Ezer's sons were Bilhan, Zaavan, and Akan.
text: Bilhann, ni Saafann, ni Akann nan ye binibaam la, le Eser nan ye biyaaja.
translated: Bilhann, and Saafann, and Akann were sons, but Eser was a daughter.

ref: Joseph's brothers were jealous of him, but his father kept wondering about the dream. Joseph Is Sold and Taken to Egypt
text: Le Josef aakpetiib nan kpa lipiipoln u pu; ute ma nan bi dakl tibɔr ngbaan pu n‑yoonn mɔmɔk. Baah di Josef kooh bi di u buen Ijipt aatiŋ ni pu na aabɔr
translated: And Joseph's brothers came and bowed down before him with their faces to the ground. Joseph saw his brothers and he recognized them, but he pretended to be a stranger to them and spoke harshly to them. And they said to Joseph that they had come to buy food in Egypt because there was famine in their land.

ref: Reuben heard this and tried to protect Joseph from them. “Let's not kill him,” he said.
text: Rubenn nan ŋun baah len pu na, le ki pɔɔn ubaa ke u ba gaa Josef lii. U nan bui bi ke, “Taa cha ti ku u.
translated: When they saw that their father was dead, they were afraid that Joseph would take revenge on them. So they sent a message to him saying, "Before our father died, he told us,

ref: When Reuben returned to the well and did not find Joseph there, he tore his clothes in sorrow.
text: Tɔ, Rubenn nan dan nnyunbun ngbaan chee ki lik, kaa kan Josef mu ponn ni, le u chuu waawanpeenkaan daa kar mpombiin pu,
translated: So, Reuben and the other brothers agreed not to kill him, but to sell Joseph, and he became a slave in a foreign land,

ref: Jacob mourned for Joseph a long time, and to show his sorrow he tore his clothes and wore sackcloth.
text: Le Jakob aasui bii, le u chuu waawanpeenkaan daa kar mpombiin pu, le ki di tiwanpeenkaan ni saak kuub na di peen. U nan wii waabo ngbaan aakun pu ni yunn pam.
translated: When Jacob was old, he could no longer see well, and his eyes were dim so he could not see. He called his eldest son to come to him.

ref: About that time Judah left his brothers in the hill country and went to live near his friend Hirah in the town of Adullam.
text: N‑yoonn mue na, Juda nan nyan ute aabim biken chee, ki buen ti bi uja ubaa chee, bi yin u ke Hira, u nan ye Adulam aatiŋ aanii la.
translated: At that time, Judah left his brothers and went down to stay with a man named Hira, who was an Adullamite.

ref: Judah asked his friend Hirah take a goat to the woman, so he could get back the ring and walking stick, but she wasn't there.
text: Le Juda di uŋobo tii ujɔ Hira u ti pa lipiiyal na waapɔln aan ki gaa ni waawan na mɔmɔk; le ujɔ Hira aa ti ki kan lipiiyal na.
translated: But Judah sent the young goat by his friend Hira to get back the pledge from the woman's hand, but he did not find the woman.

ref: Tamar later gave birth to twins. But before either of them was born, one of them stuck a hand out of her womb. The woman who was helping tied a red thread around the baby's hand and explained, “This one came out first.”
text: Buyoonn imalween nan joo u na, le bijaam ngbaan ubaa di uŋaal mbaa pue loln ni nsan ki nyan ni, le umamaan chuu uŋaal ngbaan ki di kikokomaŋ gbin mu, le ki bui ke, “Umina le puen nyan ni lipaal.”
translated: When the time came for her to give birth, there were twin boys in her womb and they were struggling with each other inside, and one put his hand out first and the midwife took it and tied a red thread on it, saying, "This one came out first."

ref: I was kidnapped from the land of the Hebrews, and here in Egypt I haven't done anything to deserve being thrown in jail.
text: Maah bi do na, bi tɔk mi Hiibru yaab aatiŋ ni la, le maah bi Ijipt aatiŋ ni do na, maa ŋa nibaa le bi di mi ŋa kiyondiik ni.”
translated: When they came, they spoke to me in the Hebrew language, but when they were in Egypt, I was the one who taught them that language.

ref: Two years later the king of Egypt dreamed he was standing beside the Nile River.
text: Ŋibin ŋilee aapuwɔb na, Ijipt aabɔr nan daŋ tidaŋ ke u si Nail aamɔɔl aagbaan,
translated: When the Israelites were in Egypt, they lived in the land near the Nile River,

ref: One night we both had dreams, and each dream had a different meaning.
text: le timɔk bilee nan daŋ tidaŋ kinyeek kibaa; ti mu aatataa aa nan kpaa.
translated: and the people of the world will see God's glory; they will know that he is the one who does it.

ref: Joseph replied: Your Majesty, both of your dreams mean the same thing, and in them God has shown what he is going to do.
text: Le Josef bui ubɔr ngbaan ke, “Tidaŋ tilee ngbaan aatataa ye tiwanbaan la. Uwumbɔr le bui si waah ban u ŋa tiwan ni na.
translated: When Joseph heard this news, he said, "This matter is very difficult for us to handle. God will help us so that we can handle it."

ref: God has given you two dreams to let you know that he has definitely decided to do this and that he will do it soon.
text: Le saah daŋ njan aadaŋ ki ki gir daŋ lelee aan tidaŋ ngbaan aatataa ye tiwanbaan na, Uwumbɔr le sil siin ti kina, ki mu ga cha ni ŋa kina dandana wee.
translated: And when they saw that they could not overcome the people of that land, Uwumbɔr took his people and left, and they went to settle in another place.

ref: Give them the power to collect the grain during those good years and to store it in your cities.
text: Bi chuun ki kuun tijikaar bil buyoonn ti ga li bi sakpen na, ki li kii ti ntim ntim ni.
translated: They should not give their daughters to people who are not of their own kind, and it should not be done at all.

ref: The king told Joseph, “God is the one who has shown you these things. No one else is as wise as you are or knows as much as you do.
text: Le ubɔr bui Josef ke, “Uwumbɔr le mɔk si tiwan mɔmɔk, nima pu na, sin le ga li kpa nlan ni nnyim ki jer unii mɔmɔk.
translated: The cupbearer said to Joseph, "My lord, I had a dream, and in it I saw a vine with three branches."
